text:
Alice: the onboarding flow is too long. we should drop the team-size question and skip the optional profile step.
Bob: agreed. one entry point, no branches.
Alice: decision: onboarding has a single entry point.
decisionTitle: onboarding has a single entry point
decisionRationale: branches fragment analytics and reduce activation

text:
Customer: i do not trust magic links for internal admin tools.
PM: noted, but for the public app we are staying with magic links. no local passwords.
Customer: ok for public app.
decisionTitle: auth uses magic links, no passwords
decisionRationale: passwords create support burden and shared-terminal risk

text:
CTO: we have one payment gateway. keep it that way until we have a second market.
PM: Stripe handles tax and dunning.
CTO: decision: billing runs through Stripe only.
decisionRationale: one gateway keeps finance, tax, and dunning consistent

text:
Designer: the export button should be disabled while the async job runs.
Engineer: i will block the UI on the polling state.
Designer: no spinner forever; cap polling at thirty seconds and surface an error.
decisionTitle: async export polls for thirty seconds max
decisionRationale: unbounded spinners hide failures and frustrate users

text:
Team: last sprint we shipped a feature that contradicted the offline-first decision because no one saw the thread.
Action: add drift checks to CI so a pull request is blocked if it violates a decided fact.
decisionTitle: CI blocks pull requests that violate decided facts
decisionRationale: humans miss threads; automated checks do not
